metadata.name: baseline_threshold_2016_narrow
metadata.description:
Narrows the economic_split threshold for v27 victory in the 2016-block retarget regime with the BASELINE (linear) price oracle.
MOTIVATION: econ_threshold_2016_v2 showed:
  econ=0.55 → v27_dominant (5,237 v27 blocks, 1,543 v26 blocks, cascade t=8,429s)
  econ=0.60 → v27_dominant (identical)

targeted_sweep11 showed:
  econ=0.50 → v26_dominant (197 v27 blocks)

The threshold is bracketed at (0.50, 0.55). This sweep tests econ=0.51 and 0.52 to narrow that bracket to ±0.01.
Research question: Is the bifurcation at econ≈0.51, 0.52, or somewhere between? The sharp jump (197 v27 blocks at 0.50 vs 5237 at 0.55) suggests a true phase transition — a small change in economic split flips the entire outcome.
MECHANISM: At econ=0.50, both chains start with identical price → no initial advantage for either neutral pool direction → standard cascade should favor v27 (it has committed pools). But the cascade at t≈8,426s is triggered by the retarget, and the retarget fires when v26 mines 2016 blocks. At econ=0.50, v26's neutral bloc preferences are equal and v26 reaches 2016 blocks first (it starts with more hashrate? — need to verify). At econ=0.51+, a tiny initial v27 price edge may shift neutral pools just enough to slow v26's block accumulation below the 2016-block threshold before the committed v26 pools give up.
PRICE GAP AT START:
  econ=0.50: gap = 0% (perfectly balanced)
  econ=0.51: gap ≈ 0.8% v27 premium  (econ_f_v27=1.004, econ_f_v26=0.996)
  econ=0.52: gap ≈ 1.6% v27 premium  (econ_f_v27=1.008, econ_f_v26=0.992)

PRIOR RESULTS FOR CONTEXT:
  econ=0.50 (2016-block, baseline, sweep_11):       v26_dominant, 197 v27 blocks
  econ=0.55 (2016-block, baseline, econ_threshold_2016_v2): v27_dominant, 5237 v27 blocks

Run across TWO namespaces (one scenario each) for parallel execution:

  Namespace A — scenario 0 (econ=0.51):
    python 3_run_sweep.py \
      --input baseline_threshold_2016_narrow/build_manifest.json \
      --namespace sweep-base-narrow-a \
      --results-dir baseline_threshold_2016_narrow/results \
      --duration 13000 --retarget-interval 2016 --interval 2 \
      --scenarios sweep_0000

  Namespace B — scenario 1 (econ=0.52):
    python 3_run_sweep.py \
      --input baseline_threshold_2016_narrow/build_manifest.json \
      --namespace sweep-base-narrow-b \
      --results-dir baseline_threshold_2016_narrow/results \
      --duration 13000 --retarget-interval 2016 --interval 2 \
      --scenarios sweep_0001

Namespaces to create first:
  kubectl create namespace sweep-base-narrow-a
  kubectl create namespace sweep-base-narrow-b

Wall-clock estimate:
  Each namespace: 1 scenario × 13,000s = ~3.6h
  Overall: ~3.6h (fully parallel)

NOTE: Both namespaces write to the same results dir (different scenario subdirs).
